commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=963f4c0473a5d997864609623fd39a70c65f46c3)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the block reply coalescer to merge buffered text into a media payload and updates direct coalescer unit coverage for combined and media-only payloads.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +23. Total +32 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path through `createBlockReplyPipeline` with coalescing enabled, where text is buffered and then flushed before a media payload is sent directly. I did not run live Discord reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the PR requires a production-path code change and contributor-supplied real Discord proof before merge.

target.title: fix(coalescer): merge buffered text into media payload instead of splitting
target.body:
When an assistant response contains both text and a `MEDIA:` attachment, the block reply coalescer flushes them as two separate Discord messages: text first, media second. Discord's API supports sending text + file in a single message, so the coalescer should merge them.

Fixes #86446

## Summary

In `createBlockReplyCoalescer`, when a media payload arrives and the buffer has text, merge the buffered text into the media payload's `content` field instead of flushing the buffer as a separate message first. Media-only payloads (no buffered text) pass through unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: text + media assistant replies send as two Discord messages instead of one
Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24.2.0, local OpenClaw dev checkout
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm test src/channels/discord-channel/block-reply-coalescer.test.ts && pnpm check:changed
Evidence after fix: terminal output from running the coalescer test suite after the patch:
```
$ pnpm test src/channels/discord-channel/block-reply-coalescer.test.ts
 ✓ |unit-fast| src/channels/discord-channel/block-reply-coalescer.test.ts (69 tests) 10ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  69 passed (69)

$ pnpm check:changed
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```
The updated test verifies the key behavior change: when text is buffered and a media payload arrives, the coalescer now merges the text into the media payload's `content` field and emits a single flush instead of two.

Observed result after fix: buffered text merges into media payload content field, producing one combined message instead of two separate messages
What was not tested: live Discord channel send (requires bot token and active server)
